provisions.content: This Proclamation may be cited as the Establishment of Districts Proclamation

Tanzania shall be divided into the Districts mentioned in the first column of the Schedule hereto, the said Districts shall be known by the names therein assigned to them and each District shall include and consist of the area or areas now known and officially recognized for purposes of administration by the name or names set opposite to the name of that District in the second column of the said Schedule. Schedule District Area Arumeru G.N. 41 of 1974 The area of the Arusha District as on 31st December, 1973, excluding that portion of that area as so previously constituted which is now included in the new Arusha Urban District constituted by proclamation contained in G.N. 1974 No. 41. Arusha Urban G.N. 41 of 1974 The area contained within the following boundaries-Commencing at a point in the Songeti Valley 460 yards north of the Arusha/Nairobi Road, the boundary runs in a south westerly direction in a bearing of 224 degrees (magnetic) to where it meets the Selian River; thence in southerly direction down the Selian to its confluence with the Burka River; thence along the Burka downstream to where it meets the Arusha/Oljoro Road; thence in a south easterly direction and in a straight line to the summit of the Mueriet Hill; thence in an easterly direction in a bearing of 97 degrees (magnetic) to the confluence of the Burka and Shem River; thence along the Shem in a south easterly direction to its confluence with the Kijenge River; thence in a southerly direction along the Kijenge River to its confluence with an unnamed stream which flows from the Loibon Hill; thence in a straight line to the summit of the Loibon Hill; thence in north easterly direction to the summit of the Losilwa Hill; thence in north north easterly direction to the railway crossing on the old Arusha/Moshi Road; thence in a north and easterly direction along the railway to where it meets the Nduruma River; thence in a north westerly direction up the Nduruma to the bridge on the Arusha/Moshi Road; thence in the same direction along the Nduruma for 200 yards, thence in a westerly direction along the Arusha/Moshi Road to where it meets the Emolosiopi Stream; thence in a north westerly direction in a bearing of 294 degrees (magnetic) to a point in the Songeti Valley, the point of commencement. Babati G.N. 234 of 1986 The area contained within the following boundaries- Commencing at the confluence of the Dongobesh River with Gadag Mbuga northwest of Durgrob Hill; thence in a north easterly direction to Gharaghara Hill; thence in a northerly direction to Gejel Hill; thence in an easterly direction to Qawirang Tlaa Hill; thence in a north easterly direction to Leya Trig. Point 7927 feet in the Nou Forest Reserve; thence north easterly along the Magara escarpment to the foot of the Magara escarpment at the northern boundary of L.O. No. 18329; thence north eastwards along this boundary to a point where it meets the southern boundary of Hassama Forest Reserve; thence the boundary follows a southerly and north easterly direction along the common boundary made by the Forest Reserve and L.O. Nos. 18329, 17792, 15826, 16096 and 15894 to the end of the common boundary at survey beacon LB 462; thence along the northern boundary to the source of the stream to its confluence with the Mutungurir River; thence down the Mutungurir River to the south western corner of L.O. No. 15517; thence along the western boundaries of L.O. NO. 15358 and 15584 meets the Marang Forest Reserve boundary at survey beacon LB 670; thence northwards along the Forest Reserve boundary to the shore of Lake Manyara; thence northwards and eastwards along the western and northern Lake shores to the estuary of the Kiruruma River; thence in an easterly and southerly direction along the eastern shore of Lake Manyara to a point where the boundary crosses the mouth of the Oltukai River where it runs into Lake Manyara; thence following the course of the Oltukai River in an easterly direction the Bridge on the Great North Road at Kwakuchinja; thence in a straight line in a south easterly direction to Oldoinyo Lol-Guberi; thence westwards on a bearing of 224 degrees to a beacon (1) in the Mbuga approx. 5 miles east of Harui; thence westwards on a bearing of 294 degrees to a beacon (2) on the northside of a clump of eucalyptus trees (Mnenura); thence in a straight line approximately 291 degrees to a beacon (3) at the south eastern end of the Wantlai stream gully; thence following the gully to where it is crossed by the Kikole Gallapo Road; thence for approximately ½ a mile on a bearing of 280 degrees to a beacon (4) thence on a bearing of 210 degrees to beacon (5); thence continuing in the same direction to a beacon (6); thence following the Kikole-Bareko track in a southerly direction for about ½ a mile to a beacon (7); thence for about a mile south west to a beacon (8) on the Yere-Awak River; thence down a stream along the Yere-Awak River to its confluence with the Bubu River; thence in a northerly direction to a point Dasego 6020 feet; thence in a northerly direction to the confluence of the Endabeg River with an unnamed stream; thence in a straight line in a westerly direction to Massanda Depression; thence in a straight line and in a southerly direction to Ghariobhally mountain; thence in a straight line due west to a point on the Giting-Bashment Road approximately 1¾ miles north of Giting; thence in the same direction to the confluence of the Endametke River with an unnamed stream; thence down the Endametke River to its mouth; thence due east of the Endametke River mouth to a point on the shore of the lake; thence southwards, westwards and northwards along the shore of the lake to the estuary of Bukaji River in Lake Balangida; thence up the Bukaji River to its source; thence in a north easterly direction to Dagharasi Hill; thence in the same direction to Sipu Hill; thence in a westerly direction and in a straight line to a point on the Dongobesh River due west of Dilojig Hill; thence up the Dongobesh River to its confluence with the Gadag mbuga northwest of Dugrob Hill, the point of commencement. Bagamoyo G.N. 178 of 1980 The area contained within the following boundaries- Commencing at the junction of the Mziha and Lukigura Rivers; thence by a straight line to a point approximately 5 miles N.W Runada; thence downstream the Mligaji River to a point 9 miles south-east of Kwamsisi; thence along the Mligaji River to its mouth in the Indian Ocean; thence seaward parallel with the coast to the Mpiji to the Vikawe Village; thence in a westerly southerly and northerly direction through Kisiwe, Mbawa and Vikuruti Villages to the Ruvu River; thence along the Ruvu River for 10 kms to the crossing with the Morogoro Road; thence down the Ruvu River to its confluence with the Msua River; thence upstream along the Msua River crossing the Mnyusi Railway line to the confluence of the Msua River with the Kisuka River; thence along the Kiaka to the junction with the old German Road Ngurungu-Bagamoyo thence west to Kinonko Station SSW to Kwa Majembe 2½ miles; thence up the Ngerengere River to its striking by a straight line due east of Kuno; thence easterly along the Wami River for 1½ miles to its confluence with the Lukigura River; thence up Lukigura River to its confluence with the Mziha River the point of commencement. Bariadi G.N. 266 of 1975 The area contained within the following boundaries- Commencing at beacon SNP 9 about 13 kms. west of Nyamagati Hill; thence in a south westerly direction to beacon SNP 19 about 8 kms. from the bank of Olgedyu Oldvai River; thence in a south westerly direction to beacon SNP 24 about ¾ km. from the bank of Lake Lgarya; thence in a south south easterly direction to beacon SNP 27 at the confluence of Olgedyu Oldvai River with an unnamed stream; thence along the south west bank of Lake Lgarya to beacon SNP 28; thence north westerly for about 25 kms. to beacon SNP 33; thence southwards for about 11 kms. to beacon SNP 26; thence westwards to the source of the Nyambono River, south of Soil O Ngum Kopjes; thence in the same direction along the Nyambono to its confluence with the Simiyu River; thence along the Simiyu River downstream to its confluence with an unnamed stream at a pathway intersected by the Simiyu, about 4 kms. due east of Ijunja Village; thence north north-eastwards for about 19 kms. to Nguduma River; thence down the Nguduma for about 10 kms. thence due north passing right between the Lumoa Hill (4127) and Nyambono Hill (4191) and proceeding northwards along the foot of Nyambono Hill to Nyamitende Hill; thence north eastwards following the centre of the Kasisi mbuga to a point on the Duma River where it meets the middle of the Kasisi mbuga; thence up the Duma to its confluence with its tributary (Nasamo River thence north easterly for nearly 3 km. to a point on a road 3 km. north west of Ngasamo; thence north westerly to a major road junction of the Ikoma/Handajenga road 8 kms. west north west of Ututwa; thence north easterly along the Ikoma/Handajenga Road to its junction with the Mbalageti River; thence, following the north bank of the Mbalageti River; thence following the north bank of the Mbalageti River to a point 13 kms. of Nyamagati Hill; thence eastwards to beacon SNP 9 the point of commencement. Biharamulo G.N. 185 of 1980 The area contained within the following boundaries- Commencing at the confluence of the Mwerusi River and Ruboroga; thence up the latter to its confluence with Kavahesi River; thence up the Kavahesi River to the valley separating Kabale and Nyakitama Villages leaving the latter in Ngara District and the former Biharamulo District to the Nyakichabo Valley; thence along the Nyakichabo Valley northwards to the Nyakichabo Valley; thence down this River to its junction with a track to the junction of old tracks; thence southeast across the western shore of the lake Burigi; thence south east for 1 mile and south for ¾ mile to the estuary of the Kasongenye River in Lake Burigi; thence up the Kasongenye for 3½ kilometres; thence due east to the summit of a hill; thence east north for 6 kilometres to another hill summit; thence in a south-easterly direction for 7 kilometres to a third hill summit thence in an easterly direction to the main Bukoba Biharamulo Road; thence along this road for 2 miles to the fork; thence in a south easterly direction through Nyamichuma Hill and Mtukula Hill; thence due east to a point just south of Chakazimbe Island; thence in a southerly direction down the middle of the Ruwondo channel through Emin Pasha Gulf to a point midway between Byasarara point and Mirumbe point; thence southwesterly and southerly passing through the middle of Bukome Bay to the estuary of the Kabunso River; thence up the Kabunso to its source 5 miles from its crossing by a path; thence south westerly to the source of Nikonga River; thence down the Nikonga River to its confluence with Bulere River at latitude 3°12' South and Longitude 32°00' East; thence up the Mwenukusi and Mhama River; thence due west to the Mwenukusi River; thence down the Mhama River 10 miles; thence due west for approximately 2½ miles to a point 5 miles up Bulere River crossing over 3 old paths to its junction with the Ulembera stream; thence up this stream to an old German road; thence for 3 miles along this road westwards to the Bisi River; thence following the Bisi River for approximately 8 miles to the confluence of the Moyowosi River to its confluence with Mwerusi River; thence up the Mwerusi to its confluence with the Ruboroga the point of commencement. Bukoba G.N. 185 of 1980 The area contained within the following boundaries- Commencing at a point on the Kagera River midway between Boundary Pillars 26 and 27 on the Tanzania-Uganda International Boundary; thence due east following the international boundary along the 1° South latitude to Lake Victoria; thence in a curve keeping 1 mile distance from the land north of Rubafu pro
